excluded_defs.name: SendFileFallback
excluded_defs.reason: read for context; the actual fix is applied in SendFileResponseExtensions because SendFileFallback only takes a Stream and has no access to response headers

exploration_log.aha_moment: SendFileFallback.SendFileAsync only accepts a Stream — it can't set headers. The fix must go in SendFileResponseExtensions.SendFileAsyncCore which has access to HttpResponse, and which is the bridge between the public API and the fallback stream copy.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Only takes a Stream destination; cannot access response headers. Task description says 'fix SendFileFallback' but the implementation surface is in SendFileResponseExtensions.

exploration_log.hindsight: Should have started with tracing callers of SendFileFallback before reading the file itself.

key_decisions.decision: Set Content-Length in SendFileResponseExtensions.SendFileAsyncCore where HttpResponse is available
key_decisions.reasoning: SendFileResponseExtensions has direct access to HttpResponse and is the entry point for both file path and IFileInfo send operations; modifying SendFileFallback's signature would be a breaking change

search_sequence.result: Only takes Stream; no access to response headers. Realized fix must be higher up.

search_sequence.reasoning: Find where in the call stack we have access to HttpResponse
search_sequence.result: StreamResponseBodyFeature.SendFileAsync calls SendFileFallback; SendFileResponseExtensions calls response.SendFileAsync

search_sequence.reasoning: The only caller that has HttpResponse
search_sequence.result: SendFileAsyncCore(HttpResponse, string) is where Content-Length must be set before the send

queries.query_text: Fix SendFileFallback to set Content-Length header from file length when response has not started and no TransferEncoding is configured

solve_notes: The `SendFileFallback.SendFileAsync` only accepts a `Stream` and has no access to response headers. The fix was applied to `SendFileResponseExtensions.SendFileAsyncCore` (both the `IFileInfo` and `string fileName` overloads), which do have access to `HttpResponse`. Before sending, the code checks `!response.HasStarted` and no `TransferEncoding` header is set, then sets `response.ContentLength` from the known file length minus offset (or uses the explicit `count` if provided).
task_text: `SendFileFallback` in `src/Http/Http/src/SendFileFallback.cs` streams file content to the response but does not set the `Content-Length` header when the file length is known. This forces chunked transfer encoding even for small files. Set `Content-Length` from the file length when the response has not yet started and no other transfer encoding is configured.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers both edited methods. The thrash-preventing set adds SendFileFallback (to understand why the fix can't go there) and StreamResponseBodyFeature.SendFileAsync (the intermediate caller) — an AI agent would naturally check these to understand the call chain before deciding where to add the fix.
